$comment: Layout of File A for the realistic 10-record fixture (examples/sample_a.dat). The companion is config/layout_file_B.json. Fields with the same name across A and B are compared; fields with exclude:true drop from the hash; fields named in only one side are dropped from that segment's comparison. Segment list order is documentation only — the parser handles whatever order shows up in the actual record bytes; only the role:key segment must be first and role:end must be last in each record.

segments.name: AD01
segments.$comment: Address segment (street/city/state/zip = 52 data bytes). An AD01 BEFORE any EM01 in the record is a postal address, compared under the name AD01. An AD01 AFTER an EM01 is renamed to EMAD by the segment_aliases rule below (ADR-034). AD01 and EMAD must declare the same size (59) — the rename only changes the comparison bucket, not the bytes.

segments.name: EM01
segments.$comment: Email segment. Its presence is the trigger: once an EM01 has been seen in a record, subsequent AD01 segments in that same record are treated as EMAD.

segments.name: EMAD
segments.$comment: Logical-only address bucket for AD01-after-EM01. EMAD never appears on the wire; it exists so the engine has a declared same-sized segment (field names matching AD01) to rename post-EM01 AD01 instances into.

segments.name: TR01
segments.$comment: TR01 may appear 1..N times per record. Multiset comparison handles count + ordering automatically.

segment_aliases.$comment: Every AD01 that appears AFTER an EM01 in the same record becomes EMAD for comparison and reporting (ADR-034). wire_name and logical_name must both be declared in segments[] and share the same size; after_segment must also be declared. At most one alias per wire_name.
